ddls:
CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Full name of the application */,
  "alias" TEXT /* Short name by which the application is commonly known */,
  "description" TEXT /* High-level description of the application and its functionality */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., emerging, standard, contained, sunset, retired) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the application was created */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the application was last updated */,
  "business_unit" TEXT /* Business unit to which the application belongs */,
  "development_team" TEXT /* Team responsible for the application's development */,
  "technical_owner" TEXT /* Technical owner of the application */,
  "retirement_date" TEXT /* Planned retirement date of the application */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the application is currently active */,
  "last_deployed_date" TEXT /* Last date the application was deployed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., emerging, standard, contained, sunset, retired) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "next_status_id" INTEGER /* Next possible status after the current one */,
  "is_final" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the status is final (e.g., retired) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the application (e.g., 1.0, 2.0) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when this version was released */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in this version */,
  "build_number" TEXT /* Build number of the version */,
  "is_production" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the version is in production */,
  "bug_fixes" TEXT /* List of bug fixes included in the version */,
  "new_features" TEXT /* List of new features included in the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_application_versions_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application this dependency belongs to */,
  "dependency_name" TEXT /* Name of the dependency (e.g., a library or another application) */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of the dependency (e.g., internal, external) */,
  "dependency_version" TEXT /* Version of the dependency */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the dependency is critical for the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_application_dependencies_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_owners" (
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each owner */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application this owner belongs to */,
  "owner_name" TEXT /* Name of the owner */,
  "owner_email" TEXT /* Email address of the owner */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the owner (e.g., primary, secondary) */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the owner */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("owner_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_application_owners_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, project manager) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application this access control belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this access control belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access granted to the user (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the access control entry expires */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the access control entry is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_application_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_application_access_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application this audit log belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this audit log belongs to */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., created, updated, deleted) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser) used to perform the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application this report belongs to */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., status, dependency) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_application_reports_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application this notification belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this notification belongs to */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, in-app) */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message contained in the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the notification has been read */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date and time the notification was sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the setting is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
